claim: Pre-approving specific tool permissions (git operations, reading files, running tests) in settings.json creates the sweet spot between safety and speed without slowing down every action
actionable: Create a permissions allow-list in .claude/settings.json for safe operations (reads, tests, git) while keeping dangerous actions (package installs, API calls) gated for approval.
source_title: Every Claude Code Concept Explained for Normal People

claim: Deny rules in settings.json prevent Claude from even discovering sensitive files (API keys, secrets folders) making them completely off-limits without manual intervention
actionable: Add file deny lists to settings.json for .env files and credential folders so Claude cannot read them even if explicitly asked.
source_title: Every Claude Code Concept Explained for Normal People

claim: CLAUDE.md is a critical foundational file where you write once and Claude follows those preferences (language, coding style, documentation rules) across every conversation
actionable: Create a CLAUDE.md file at your project root documenting code standards, language choices, brand voice, and project conventions that Claude will read first each session.
source_title: Every Claude Code Concept Explained for Normal People

claim: Context window fills at 85% and auto-compacting can be triggered with /compact to remove noise while preserving key information for the next session
actionable: Use /compact command before context gets bloated, or let auto-compacting kick in at 85% threshold to keep important context and discard fluff.
source_title: Every Claude Code Concept Explained for Normal People

claim: Extended thinking is now on by default in Claude Code with a max token budget for reasoning, no need for special trigger words like 'think hard'
actionable: Trust that Claude is using its reasoning budget automatically on complex tasks; no special prompt engineering needed to enable extended thinking.
source_title: Every Claude Code Concept Explained for Normal People

claim: Sub-agents are best for self-contained tasks (analyze this report and come back) while skills are better for improving Claude's capability within the current conversation
actionable: Use sub-agents when delegating autonomous work that doesn't need real-time feedback, and skills when you need Claude better at something in the current session.
source_title: Every Claude Code Concept Explained for Normal People

claim: Agent teams solve the bottleneck of multiple sub-agents reporting only to the main agent by letting teammates communicate directly with shared task lists
actionable: Use agent teams for complex multi-part builds (API dev, frontend, testing in parallel) where sub-agents need to coordinate rather than all routing through main agent.
source_title: Every Claude Code Concept Explained for Normal People

claim: Worktrees create isolated copies of your codebase with separate branches so multiple Claude instances work on different features in parallel without interference
actionable: Use --worktree or -w flag to create isolated working directories for parallel feature branches; Claude handles cleanup after merging.
source_title: Every Claude Code Concept Explained for Normal People
